4	/**
5	 * Middleware for ImageModelV3.
6	 * This type defines the structure for middleware that can be used to modify
7	 * the behavior of ImageModelV3 operations.
8	 */
9	export type ImageModelV3Middleware = {
10	  /**
11	   * Middleware specification version. Use `v3` for the current version.
12	   */
13	  readonly specificationVersion: 'v3';
14	
15	  /**
16	   * Override the provider name if desired.
17	   * @param options.model - The image model instance.
18	   */
19	  overrideProvider?: (options: { model: ImageModelV3 }) => string;
20	
21	  /**
22	   * Override the model ID if desired.
23	   * @param options.model - The image model instance.
24	   */
25	  overrideModelId?: (options: { model: ImageModelV3 }) => string;
26	
27	  /**
28	   * Override the limit of how many images can be generated in a single API call if desired.
29	   * @param options.model - The image model instance.
30	   */
31	  overrideMaxImagesPerCall?: (options: {
32	    model: ImageModelV3;
33	  }) => ImageModelV3['maxImagesPerCall'];
34	
35	  /**
36	   * Transforms the parameters before they are passed to the image model.
37	   * @param options - Object containing the parameters.
38	   * @param options.params - The original parameters for the image model call.
39	   * @returns A promise that resolves to the transformed parameters.
40	   */
41	  transformParams?: (options: {
42	    params: ImageModelV3CallOptions;
43	    model: ImageModelV3;
44	  }) => PromiseLike<ImageModelV3CallOptions>;
45	
46	  /**
47	   * Wraps the generate operation of the image model.
48	   *
49	   * @param options - Object containing the generate function, parameters, and model.
50	   * @param options.doGenerate - The original generate function.
51	   * @param options.params - The parameters for the generate call. If the
52	   * `transformParams` middleware is used, this will be the transformed parameters.
53	   * @param options.model - The image model instance.
54	   * @returns A promise that resolves to the result of the generate operation.
55	   */
56	  wrapGenerate?: (options: {
57	    doGenerate: () => ReturnType<ImageModelV3['doGenerate']>;
58	    params: ImageModelV3CallOptions;
59	    model: ImageModelV3;
60	  }) => Promise<Awaited<ReturnType<ImageModelV3['doGenerate']>>>;
61	};

1	# AI SDK - fal Provider
2	
3	The **[fal provider](https://ai-sdk.dev/providers/ai-sdk-providers/fal)** for the [AI SDK](https://ai-sdk.dev/docs) contains image model support for the [fal.ai API](https://fal.ai/).
4	
5	## Setup
6	
7	The fal provider is available in the `@ai-sdk/fal` module. You can install it with
8	
9	```bash
10	npm i @ai-sdk/fal
11	```
12	
13	## Skill for Coding Agents
14	
15	If you use coding agents such as Claude Code or Cursor, we highly recommend adding the AI SDK skill to your repository:
16	
17	```shell
18	npx skills add vercel/ai
19	```
20	
21	## Provider Instance
22	
23	You can import the default provider instance `fal` from `@ai-sdk/fal`:
24	
25	```ts
26	import { fal } from '@ai-sdk/fal';
27	```
28	
29	## Image Generation Example
30	
31	```ts
32	import { fal } from '@ai-sdk/fal';
33	import { generateImage } from 'ai';
34	import fs from 'fs';
35	const { image } = await generateImage({
36	  model: fal.image('fal-ai/flux/schnell'),
37	  prompt: 'A cat wearing a intricate robe',
38	});
39	
40	const filename = `image-${Date.now()}.png`;
41	fs.writeFileSync(filename, image.uint8Array);
42	console.log(`Image saved to ${filename}`);
43	```
44	
45	## Additional Options
46	
47	If you want to pass additional inputs to the model besides the prompt, use the `providerOptions.fal` property:
48	
49	```ts
50	const { image } = await generateImage({
51	  model: fal.image('fal-ai/recraft-v3'),
52	  prompt: 'A cat wearing a intricate robe',
53	  size: '1920x1080',
54	  providerOptions: {
55	    fal: {
56	      style: 'digital_illustration',
57	    },
58	  },
59	});
60	```
61	
62	## Documentation
63	
64	Please check out the **[fal provider](https://ai-sdk.dev/providers/ai-sdk-providers/fal)** for more information.
65

Perfect! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive report. Let me compile all the findings:

## Complete AI SDK Provider Package Configuration Guide

I've thoroughly explored the monorepo structure and analyzed both the FAL and OpenAI provider packages. Here's the complete breakdown of what files need to be created for a new provider package and what goes in each:

---

## **1. PACKAGE.JSON STRUCTURE**

### Location: `packages/{provider-name}/package.json`

**Key Requirements:**
- Naming convention: `@ai-sdk/{provider-name}`
- Must include workspace dependencies on `@ai-sdk/provider` and `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`
- Should have `zod` as both a peer dependency and dev dependency
- Version management uses changesets (see section 6)

**Example from FAL** (`/packages/fal/package.json`):
```json
{
  "name": "@ai-sdk/fal",
  "version": "2.0.28",
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "sideEffects": false,
  "main": "./dist/index.js",
  "module": "./dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
  "files": [
    "dist/**/*",
    "docs/**/*",
    "src",
    "!src/**/*.test.ts",
    "!src/**/*.test-d.ts",
    "!src/**/__snapshots__",
    "!src/**/__fixtures__",
    "CHANGELOG.md",
    "README.md"
  ],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "pnpm clean && tsup --tsconfig tsconfig.build.json",
    "build:watch": "pnpm clean && tsup --watch",
    "clean": "del-cli dist docs *.tsbuildinfo",
    "prepack": "mkdir -p docs && cp ../../content/providers/01-ai-sdk-providers/10-fal.mdx ./docs/",
    "postpack": "del-cli docs",
    "type-check": "tsc --build",
    "test": "pnpm test:node && pnpm test:edge",
    "test:update": "pnpm test:node -u",
    "test:watch": "vitest --config vitest.node.config.js",
    "test:edge": "vitest --config vitest.edge.config.js --run",
    "test:node": "vitest --config vitest.node.config.js --run"
  },
  "exports": {
    "./package.json": "./package.json",
    ".": {
      "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/index.mjs",
      "require": "./dist/index.js"
    }
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@ai-sdk/provider": "workspace:*",
    "@ai-sdk/provider-utils": "workspace:*"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@ai-sdk/test-server": "workspace:*",
    "@types/node": "20.17.24",
    "@vercel/ai-tsconfig": "workspace:*",
    "tsup": "^8",
    "typescript": "5.8.3",
    "zod": "3.25.76"
  },
  "peerDependencies": {
    "zod": "^3.25.76 || ^4.1.8"
  },
  "engines": {
    "node": ">=18"
  },
  "publishConfig": {
    "access": "public"
  },
  "homepage": "https://ai-sdk.dev/docs",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "git+https://github.com/vercel/ai.git"
  },
  "bugs": {
    "url": "https://github.com/vercel/ai/issues"
  },
  "keywords": ["ai"]
}
```

**Notes:**
- If you need a secondary export entry (like OpenAI's `"./internal"`), add it in the exports section
- The `prepack`/`postpack` scripts copy docs from `/content/providers/` folder
- `zod` is required for schema validation of provider options

---

## **2. TSCONFIG.JSON**

### Location: `packages/{provider-name}/tsconfig.json`

**Simple structure that extends shared config:**
```json
{
  "extends": "./node_modules/@vercel/ai-tsconfig/ts-library.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "composite": true,
    "rootDir": "src",
    "outDir": "dist"
```

**Key points:**
- Extends `@vercel/ai-tsconfig/ts-library.json` (shared config)
- `"composite": true` for monorepo project references
- Always reference `../provider` and `../provider-utils`
- The base config comes from `@vercel/ai-tsconfig` workspace package

## **3. TSCONFIG.BUILD.JSON**

### Location: `packages/{provider-name}/tsconfig.build.json`

**Minimal override to disable composite mode for builds:**

```json
  "compilerOptions": {
    "composite": false
  }
}
```

**Purpose:** Used by tsup during the build process to avoid composite TypeScript project issues

---

## **4. TSUP BUILD CONFIG**

### Location: `packages/{provider-name}/tsup.config.ts`

**For simple providers (single entry point like FAL):**
```typescript
import { defineConfig } from 'tsup';

export default defineConfig([
  {
    entry: ['src/index.ts'],
    format: ['cjs', 'esm'],
    dts: true,
    sourcemap: true,
    define: {
      __PACKAGE_VERSION__: JSON.stringify(
        (await import('./package.json', { with: { type: 'json' } })).default
          .version,
      ),
    },
  },
]);
```

**For complex providers with multiple exports (like OpenAI with `./internal`):**
```typescript
import { defineConfig } from 'tsup';

export default defineConfig([
  {
    entry: ['src/index.ts'],
    format: ['cjs', 'esm'],

  },
  {
    entry: ['src/internal/index.ts'],
    outDir: 'dist/internal',
    format: ['cjs', 'esm'],
    dts: true,
    sourcemap: true,
    define: {
      __PACKAGE_VERSION__: JSON.stringify(
        (await import('./package.json', { with: { type: 'json' } })).default
          .version,
      ),
    },
  },
]);
```

**Key points:**
- Generates both CommonJS (`cjs`) and ESM (`esm`) formats
- `dts: true` generates TypeScript definitions
- `sourcemap: true` for debugging
- Version string is injected at build time from `package.json`

---

## **5. ROOT TSCONFIG.JSON - PACKAGE REGISTRATION**

### Location: `/tsconfig.json`

All packages must be registered in the root `references` array. Current state shows 54 packages, example entries:

```json
{
  "references": [
    { "path": "packages/ai" },
    { "path": "packages/fal" },
    { "path": "packages/openai" },
    // ... 51 more packages
    { "path": "packages/xai" }
  ],
  "include": []
}
```

**CRITICAL:** When adding a new provider, you MUST add an entry here in alphabetical order.

## **8. SRC/INDEX.TS - MAIN ENTRY POINT**

### FAL Example (Image Generation Provider):
```typescript
export type { FalProvider, FalProviderSettings } from './fal-provider';
export { createFal, fal } from './fal-provider';
export type {
```

**Requirements:**
1. Export a default provider instance (e.g., `fal`)
2. Export a factory function (e.g., `createFal`)
3. Export the provider type and settings type
4. Export all model option types for each model type the provider supports
5. Export VERSION constant (defined elsewhere, typically `./version.ts`)
6. Maintain deprecated type aliases for backwards compatibility

---

## **9. PROVIDER IMPLEMENTATION PATTERN**

### File Structure for Image Generation Provider:
```
packages/fal/src/
├── index.ts                    # Main exports
├── version.ts                  # Version constant
├── fal-provider.ts            # Factory function & default instance
├── fal-provider.test.ts       # Provider tests
├── fal-image-model.ts         # ImageModelV3 implementation
├── fal-image-model.test.ts    # Image model tests
├── fal-image-options.ts       # Zod schema for image options
├── fal-image-settings.ts      # Model IDs and settings types
├── fal-speech-model.ts        # SpeechModelV3 implementation
├── fal-speech-model.test.ts   # Speech tests

├── fal-video-model.ts         # VideoModelV3 implementation

├── fal-api-types.ts           # API-specific type definitions
├── fal-error.ts               # Error handling
├── fal-config.ts              # Configuration
├── __fixtures__/              # Test fixtures
│   ├── fal-transcription-result.json
│   └── fal-transcription-queue.json
└── transcript-test.mp3        # Test audio file
```

### Image Model Implementation Pattern:
The `FalImageModel` class implements `ImageModelV3`:
- Constructor takes `modelId` and `config` (with baseURL, headers, fetch function)
- Implements `doGenerate()` method with specific signature from provider interface
- Uses utility functions from `@ai-sdk/provider-utils`:
  - `parseProviderOptions()` - validates provider-specific options with Zod
  - `postJsonToApi()` - makes API requests
  - `createJsonResponseHandler()` - handles responses
  - `convertImageModelFileToDataUri()` - converts image files to data URIs

### Location: `packages/{provider-name}/README.md`

Standard format:
```markdown
# AI SDK - {Provider} Provider

The **[{provider} provider](https://ai-sdk.dev/providers/...)** for the [AI SDK](https://ai-sdk.dev/docs) contains support for the [{provider} API](...).

## Setup

The {provider} provider is available in the `@ai-sdk/{provider}` module.

\`\`\`bash
npm i @ai-sdk/{provider}
\`\`\`

## Provider Instance

\`\`\`ts
import { {provider} } from '@ai-sdk/{provider}';
\`\`\`

## Example

[Example code showing how to use the provider for each capability]

## Documentation

[Link to detailed docs]
```

---

## **12. CRITICAL REGISTRATION CHECKLIST**

To add a new provider package, you MUST:

1. **Create package directory**: `packages/{provider-name}/`
2. **Add to root tsconfig.json**: Add entry in `references` array (alphabetical order)
3. **Add to root turbo.json**: Provider is automatically included via `globalEnv`
4. **Create all config files**: `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `tsconfig.build.json`, `tsup.config.ts`, `turbo.json`, `vitest.*.config.js`
5. **Create version.ts**: Define VERSION export
6. **Create provider factory**: Main provider class + factory function
7. **Implement model classes**: One class per model type (Language, Image, Speech, etc.)
8. **Create option schemas**: Zod schemas for all provider-specific options
9. **Export from index.ts**: All types and functions needed by users
10. **Update CHANGELOG.md**: Add initial entry
11. **Create README.md**: Document setup and usage
12. **Add tests**: Minimum test files for provider and each model type

---

## **13. INHERITED INTERFACES**

All providers implement interfaces from `@ai-sdk/provider`:
- `LanguageModelV3` - text generation
- `ImageModelV3` - image generation/editing (like FAL)
- `SpeechModelV3` - speech synthesis
- `TranscriptionModelV3` - audio-to-text (like FAL)
- `EmbeddingModelV3` - text embeddings
- `VideoModelV3` - video generation (experimental)

All share utility functions from `@ai-sdk/provider-utils` for:
- HTTP requests (postJsonToApi, getFromApi)
- Response handling (createJsonResponseHandler, etc.)
- Error handling (createJsonErrorResponseHandler)
- Data conversion (convertImageModelFileToDataUri)

---

## **SUMMARY: FILES TO CREATE FOR NEW PROVIDER**

```
packages/{provider-name}/
├── package.json                    [standard structure above]
├── tsconfig.json                   [extends @vercel/ai-tsconfig]
├── tsconfig.build.json             [disables composite]
├── tsup.config.ts                  [build config]
├── turbo.json                       [cache settings]
├── vitest.node.config.js           [node tests]
├── vitest.edge.config.js           [edge tests]
├── README.md                        [documentation]
├── CHANGELOG.md                     [versioning]
└── src/
    ├── index.ts                    [main exports]
    ├── version.ts                  [VERSION constant]
    ├── {provider}-provider.ts      [factory & instance]
    ├── {provider}-provider.test.ts [provider tests]
    ├── {provider}-[type]-model.ts  [one per capability]
    ├── {provider}-[type]-options.ts [Zod schemas]
    ├── {provider}-[type]-settings.ts [IDs & types]
    ├── {provider}-error.ts         [error handling]
    ├── {provider}-config.ts        [config types]
    ├── {provider}-api-types.ts     [API defs]
    └── __fixtures__/               [test data]

ROOT CHANGES NEEDED:
├── tsconfig.json                   [add reference to package]
└── (turbo.json - already includes all providers)
```

---

This monorepo uses a highly standardized structure that scales to 54+ providers. Each provider package is essentially a template copy with provider-specific implementation details.

### Location: `packages/{provider-name}/README.md`

Standard format:
```markdown
# AI SDK - {Provider} Provider

The **[{provider} provider](https://ai-sdk.dev/providers/...)** for the [AI SDK](https://ai-sdk.dev/docs) contains support for the [{provider} API](...).

## Setup

The {provider} provider is available in the `@ai-sdk/{provider}` module.

\`\`\`bash
npm i @ai-sdk/{provider}
\`\`\`

## Provider Instance

\`\`\`ts
import { {provider} } from '@ai-sdk/{provider}';
\`\`\`

## Example

[Example code showing how to use the provider for each capability]

## Documentation

[Link to detailed docs]
```

---

## **12. CRITICAL REGISTRATION CHECKLIST**

To add a new provider package, you MUST:

1. **Create package directory**: `packages/{provider-name}/`
2. **Add to root tsconfig.json**: Add entry in `references` array (alphabetical order)
3. **Add to root turbo.json**: Provider is automatically included via `globalEnv`
4. **Create all config files**: `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `tsconfig.build.json`, `tsup.config.ts`, `turbo.json`, `vitest.*.config.js`
5. **Create version.ts**: Define VERSION export
6. **Create provider factory**: Main provider class + factory function
7. **Implement model classes**: One class per model type (Language, Image, Speech, etc.)
8. **Create option schemas**: Zod schemas for all provider-specific options
9. **Export from index.ts**: All types and functions needed by users
10. **Update CHANGELOG.md**: Add initial entry
11. **Create README.md**: Document setup and usage
12. **Add tests**: Minimum test files for provider and each model type

---

## **13. INHERITED INTERFACES**

All providers implement interfaces from `@ai-sdk/provider`:
- `LanguageModelV3` - text generation
- `ImageModelV3` - image generation/editing (like FAL)
- `SpeechModelV3` - speech synthesis
- `TranscriptionModelV3` - audio-to-text (like FAL)
- `EmbeddingModelV3` - text embeddings
- `VideoModelV3` - video generation (experimental)

All share utility functions from `@ai-sdk/provider-utils` for:
- HTTP requests (postJsonToApi, getFromApi)
- Response handling (createJsonResponseHandler, etc.)
- Error handling (createJsonErrorResponseHandler)
- Data conversion (convertImageModelFileToDataUri)

---

## **SUMMARY: FILES TO CREATE FOR NEW PROVIDER**

```
packages/{provider-name}/
├── package.json                    [standard structure above]
├── tsconfig.json                   [extends @vercel/ai-tsconfig]
├── tsconfig.build.json             [disables composite]
├── tsup.config.ts                  [build config]
├── turbo.json                       [cache settings]
├── vitest.node.config.js           [node tests]
├── vitest.edge.config.js           [edge tests]
├── README.md                        [documentation]
├── CHANGELOG.md                     [versioning]
└── src/
    ├── index.ts                    [main exports]
    ├── version.ts                  [VERSION constant]
    ├── {provider}-provider.ts      [factory & instance]
    ├── {provider}-provider.test.ts [provider tests]
    ├── {provider}-[type]-model.ts  [one per capability]
    ├── {provider}-[type]-options.ts [Zod schemas]
    ├── {provider}-[type]-settings.ts [IDs & types]
    ├── {provider}-error.ts         [error handling]
    ├── {provider}-config.ts        [config types]
    ├── {provider}-api-types.ts     [API defs]
    └── __fixtures__/               [test data]

ROOT CHANGES NEEDED:
├── tsconfig.json                   [add reference to package]
└── (turbo.json - already includes all providers)
```

---

This monorepo uses a highly standardized structure that scales to 54+ providers. Each provider package is essentially a template copy with provider-specific implementation details.
